title: salvia officinalis subsp. multiflora
intro: Salvia officinalis subsp. multiflora, also known as multiflorous sage, is a subspecies of the Salvia officinalis plant, a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, blue flowers and grayish-green leaves. The medicinal properties of Salvia officinalis subsp. multiflora include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating wounds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The health benefits of consuming Salvia officinalis subsp. multiflora include improving cognitive function, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-aging properties. Salvia officinalis subsp. multiflora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
